title: Vector Store Schema, Versioning, Tenancy, Updates, and Deletes
description: A vector store keeps only embedding and text, overwrites IDs on retry, mixes tenants, cannot identify its model revision, and leaves deleted content searchable. Learners cumulatively produce versioned vector-record lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define document, chunk, tenant, source, version, offsets, content hash, model, dimensions, vector, metadata, timestamps, and policy fields.
Reject queries when model, dimensions, preprocessing, metric, index, or schema versions are incompatible.
Make ingestion and reindexing idempotent with stable keys, generation identity, checkpoints, compare-and-swap, and reconciliation.
